item.title: 2 arrested over construction material theft
item.description: Two employees of Bangladesh Medical University’s Super Specialised Hospital were arrested on Wednesday over stealing of construction equipment and materials from the hospital......

item.title: BMU, Kunming Medical Univ sign memo to launch joint surgical clinic
item.description: Bangladesh Medical University signed a memorandum of understanding with the Kunming Medical University of China at the BMU in Dhaka on Wednesday to launch a joint surgical clinic......

item.title: Govt should introduce people-friendly energy policy: discussion
item.description: Politicians, academics and professionals on Wednesday urged the government to introduce a people-friendly policy to ensure the country’s energy security......

item.title: Professor Nazrul Islam made BMU pro-VC
item.description: Professor Dr Md Nazrul Islam has been appointed as new pro-vice-chancellor, academic, of the Bangladesh Medical University......

item.title: Chhatra Dal protests, Shibir seeks action
item.description: Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Chhatra Dal and Bangladesh Islami Chhatra Shibir traded blame for the Tuesday clash at Government City College in Chattogram city......

item.title: Five more die of suspected measles
item.description: Five more people died across the country due to suspected measles in the last 24 hours, said a press release of the Directorate General of Health Services on Wednesday......

item.title: 4 killed in Russia, Ukraine after exchange of strikes
item.description: Four people, including a child, were killed in Russia and Ukraine after the latest exchange of drone strikes between the warring countries, officials said on Wednesday......

item.title: Ziauddin, Saimum appointed PM’s special assistants
item.description: The prime minister has appointed SM Ziauddin Hyder, a former senior health specialist at the World Bank, as his special assistant on health affairs with the status of a state minister and Saimum Parvez as his special assistant on environment, forests and climate change......

item.title: First phase of West Bengal vote today
item.description: A total of 152 assembly constituencies will go to the polls today in West Bengal under the first phase of Assembly election covering 16 districts, reports newsonair.gov.in......

item.title: 12 Jamaat alliance candidates valid for reserved seats
item.description: The election commission has declared the nomination papers of 12 candidates from the Jamaat-e-Islami-led alliance valid for the reserved women’s seat election, while keeping the nomination of one candidate pending......Newspapers

item.title: Fuel pass mandatory for bikers at seven pumps
item.description: The Energy Division on Wednesday made fuel pass mandatory for motorcyclists at seven city filling stations in its latest move to streamline supply that has gone haywire over the past 47 days because of import disruption in the wake of a war in the Gulf region......

item.title: Justice Abdul Jabbar Khan’s 42nd death anniv today
item.description: The 42nd death anniversary of Justice Abdul Jabbar Khan, a former speaker of the then Pakistan National Assembly, will be observed today with special prayer sessions......

item.title: Lawyers threaten continuous protests over virtual court decision
item.description: Tension gripped the Supreme Court premises on Wednesday as lawyers protested a decision to operate the Appellate and High Court divisions virtually for two days a week, demanding its immediate withdrawal....

item.title: Govt to start grain procurement May 3
item.description: Home minister Salahuddin Ahmed on Wednesday said that the government would begin a nationwide drive to procure paddy, rice and wheat from May 3, continuing until August 31......

item.title: 11 invigilators, 2 centre secretaries relieved from duty
item.description: Eleven teachers on Wednesday were released from all their duties as invigilators for the ongoing Secondary School Certificate examinations over negligence of duty......

item.title: ULAB celebrates Earth Day 2026
item.description: The University of Liberal Arts Bangladesh marked a three-day celebration of Earth Day at the ULAB in Dhaka concluded on Wednesday......Geographic Reference

item.title: Hasnat wants govt car for MPs
item.description: Opposition lawmaker Hasnat Abdullah on Wednesday clarified in Jatiya Sangsad that he did not seek tax-free cars, saying that he had only proposed allowing lawmakers to use government cars during their tenure like other public servants, without incurring additional costs......

item.title: Tk 8,000cr facility remains idle
item.description: The Maheshkhali Single Point Mooring project, costing over Tk 8,000 crore and aiming at cutting import costs of fuel oils significantly, could not get over controversies and has been lying idle for almost two years in the absence of an operator, according to Energy Division officials......

item.title: When downsizing leaves a silent crisis behind
item.description: IN BOARDROOMS, downsizing is often framed as a necessary, even strategic decision. It cuts costs, improves efficiency and helps organisations stay competitive in uncertain markets. On paper, it makes sense. But behind these calculations lies a quieter, often overlooked reality: what happens to the employees who remain......

item.title: Baishakh and power
item.description: THE echoes of the dhak have faded, and the colossal papier-mâché structures have been dismantled, but the 1433 Baishakh celebration (14 April) has left a lingering unease in the corridors of Bengali culture. The centrepiece of our secular pride, the UNESCO-recognised ‘Mangal Shobhajatra’, was officially rebranded as ‘Borshoboron Ananda Shobhajatra’ in 2025......

item.title: Islam in imperial mirror
item.description: EDWARD Said was a Palestinian-American literary theorist, cultural critic, and one of the most influential intellectuals of modern thought. Born in Jerusalem and educated in the United States, he later taught at Columbia University. His work was not confined to literary analysis alone; it was a sustained effort to reveal the intimate relationship between knowledge, power, and representation......

item.title: The crack within
item.description: ALONE in a small room that houses the armoury of Khulna railway police lines, constable Samrat Biswas, 27, has one hand, supported by the other, over his mouth. After a few moments of stillness, he runs one of his clumsy fingers in the middle of where his eyebrows are apart quite distinctly and brings it down tapping over the ridge of his nose as if to remove something, maybe an insect that disturbs his deep thought......

item.title: Exploring youth crime nexus
item.description: Bangladesh stands at a critical demographic juncture. More than a third of its population is under the age of 35; a condition economists describe as a demographic dividend, a window during which a large working-age population can accelerate growth. Yet beneath this familiar narrative lies a less comfortable reality that is becoming increasingly difficult to ignore......

item.title: Offices of MPs in upazila complexes risk overreach
item.description: MEMBERS of parliament are going to have rooms in upazila complexes dedicated as office chambers so that they can strengthen their engagement with their electorates. The government has already issued an order, as the minister of state for local government said in the parliament on April 21, for upazila nirbahi officers to prepare specially designated ‘visiting rooms’ for......

item.title: Govt must deliver on promises to curb mob violence
item.description: THE rise of mob violence and attacks on shrines and sufi practitioners was among the most alarming and disturbing developments after the August 2024politicalchangeover. Mob violence became an almost daily occurrence and attacks on shrines and sufi durbars continued unabated amid visible inaction on part of the authorities. While dozens of shrines were attacked and......Politics

item.title: Farida Khanam becomes first female DC of Dhaka
item.description: The government on Wednesday appointed Farida Khanam, deputy secretary of the Ministry of Home Affairs, as the new deputy commissioner of Dhaka......

item.title: Low water shuts 4 Kaptai power units
item.description: Electricity generation at the Karnphuli Hydrpower Plant in Kaptai has declined significantly due to a fall in water levels in Kaptai Lake, with four out of five units now shut down, officials said on Wednesday......

item.title: DU takes steps to modernise student services
item.description: The Dhaka University administration in a press release on Wednesday said that the DU had recently taken various steps to modernise the service activities for students of the university......

item.title: Bangladesh chairs key session at UNESCAP in Bangkok
item.description: Science and technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam chaired the morning session at the 82nd session of the United Nations Economic and Social Commission for Asia and the Pacific in Bangkok in Thailand on Tuesday......
